YOM Bundle Recommender System
  • Data Flow
  • Edit on GitHub

Data Flow

Overview

This page consolidates the data flow described in README.md and documentation.md.

Input data

Source - Location - Description

Raw orders - training/data/raw/*.csv - Transaction data (order, kiosk, product, date, quantity)

Product catalog - training/data/external/products_v2.csv - Product metadata (id, name, category)

Kiosk metadata - training/data/external/commerces.csv - Kiosk metadata (channel, region, active flag)

Pipeline flow

Raw CSV + metadata
    -> preprocess + active-kiosk filter
    -> time split (train/val/test)
    -> baskets and MBA candidates
    -> feature table and labels
    -> LightGBM LambdaRank training
    -> model artifacts
    -> batch scoring on recent window
    -> serving parquet artifacts
    -> API serving with lookup + fallback

Intermediate artifacts

  • training/data/interim/orders_sample.parquet (preprocessed orders)

  • Temporary split batches generated by training pipeline steps

Serving artifacts

Batch scoring writes four parquet files used by serving:

  • training/data/interim/predictions.parquet

  • training/data/interim/popularity_fallback.parquet

  • training/data/interim/category_fallback.parquet

  • training/data/interim/global_fallback.parquet

Documented scale indicators

The technical documentation reports the following run profile for the 90-day inference window:

  • Orders processed: about 3.07M

  • Active kiosks with predictions: 41,337

  • Anchor products with MBA candidates: 315

  • Kiosk coverage: about 83.6%

Why this flow is used

  • Training and scoring are decoupled from serving.

  • Serving can keep low latency by reading precomputed artifacts.

  • Fallback artifacts ensure recommendation coverage for unseen request contexts.
